content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

All three amendments have technical merit. Two identify real correctness/audit regressions, and one identifies a low-severity but valid robustness/performance defect caused by unnecessary state-commit sidecar work.

## Votes

- `fr-16-d6120209`: Accept
  `commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops` creates and publishes the pending sidecar before any check for a total no-op. When `applied == 0` and `previous_snapshot == next_snapshot`, that sidecar and the follow-on writes are pure overhead. Plain readers explicitly notice pending sidecars and can take the mutation lock to run recovery, so this is a real unnecessary I/O and contention window even if it is not a correctness failure.
- `fr-16-5bcdbbf5`: Accept
  The decoder now unwraps any single-key object named `data`, but this codepath also accepts Claude payloads that are already unwrapped. That makes a legitimate payload shaped as `{ "data": ... }` ambiguous and causes destructive normalization to the inner value. Because the transport wrapper is no longer uniquely identified, this is a real structured-output shape regression for valid schemas.
- `fr-16-26354296`: Accept
  `update_status_locked` hard-codes the `Running` transition reason to `"execution started"` even though `Paused -> Running` is an allowed direct transition. The same file already distinguishes resume from start in other transition-building paths, so this direct API path records incorrect audit/journal semantics for resumes. That is a real correctness issue in lifecycle event reporting.
